data.title: EU aluminum industry, facing a 50% tariff, is under pressure. Brussels racing to protect 40 billion euros industry and 250,000 jobs. Scrap metal shipments to US exempt, causing European recyclers to suffer. Plants shutting down, risking survival.Character count: 261
data.description: President Donald Trump's fluctuating tariff policy poses a challenge for the aluminum supply chain, highlighting its vulnerabilities. With the EU enduring a 50% tariff for almost three months, urgent actions are being taken to counteract the adverse impacts. Brussels is crafting emergency strategies to safeguard its €40 billion aluminum industry, which directly employs 250,000 individuals and supports a further 1 million jobs. The existing policy asymmetry, where finished aluminum exports to the U.S. confront a 50% tariff while scrap metal shipments are spared, has led to a surplus of scrap heading to the U.S. Consequently, European recyclers are deprived of essential raw materials. The repercussions are dire, with plants cutting down operations not just to maintain profits but to sustain their operations. According to Paul Voss, European Aluminium's head, firms have only weeks to address the looming crisis. Industry experts caution that the U.S. aluminum tariffs not only jeopardize the supply chain but also food security. More comprehensive details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Only 20% of organizations are prepared for Automation and AI, according to GBTEC's research. Process excellence is crucial.
data.description: In Bochum, Germany, a recent report by GBTEC highlights that despite significant investments in AI and automation by organizations, many lack the operational maturity required for success. The report, titled The 2025 Process Excellence and AI-Readiness Report, underscores the importance of process excellence in facilitating effective business transformation through automation and AI. Based on input from 600 senior business and operations leaders in ANZ, Europe, and North America, the report sheds light on the critical role of process optimization in ensuring successful integration of technologies.

data.title: Flight canceled by United, passenger sold a new seat for $648 on the same flight after last-minute notice in Venice.
data.description: After returning from Venice, a traveler was unexpectedly informed that the last segment of his journey was canceled. Subsequently, he paid $648 to secure a new seat on the same flight.
